properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued April 1 at 12:12AM EDT until April 1 at 12:15AM EDT by NWS Pittsburgh PA
properties.description:
The tornado threat has diminished and the Tornado Warning has been
cancelled. However, damaging winds remain likely and a Severe
Thunderstorm Warning remains in effect for the area. However, heavy
rain is still possible with this thunderstorm.

NWSheadline: TORNADO WARNING WILL EXPIRE AT 1215 AM EDT FOR EAST CENTRAL ALLEGHENY AND WEST CENTRAL WESTMORELAND COUNTIES

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued April 1 at 12:00AM EDT until April 1 at 12:15AM EDT by NWS Pittsburgh PA
properties.description:
TORPBZ

The National Weather Service in Pittsburgh has issued a

* Tornado Warning for...
East Central Allegheny County in southwestern Pennsylvania...
West Central Westmoreland County in southwestern Pennsylvania...

* Until 1215 AM EDT.

* At midnight EDT, a severe thunderstorm capable of producing a
tornado was located over Duquesne, or over McKeesport, moving east
at 45 mph.

HAZARD...Tornado.

SOURCE...Radar indicated rotation.

IMPACT...Flying debris will be dangerous to those caught without
shelter. Mobile homes will be damaged or destroyed. Damage
to roofs, windows and vehicles will occur. Tree damage is
likely.

* Locations impacted include...
McKeesport, West Mifflin, Munhall, North Versailles, White Oak,
Duquesne, Glassport, Irwin, Manor, Trafford, Liberty, Port Vue,
East Mckeesport, Dravosburg, Versailles, North Irwin, Penn and
Adamsburg.
properties.instruction:
Move to an interior room on the lowest floor of a well-built building
away from windows. If you are outdoors, in a mobile home, or in a
vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter and protect yourself
from flying debris.

CMAMtext: NWS: TORNADO WARNING in this area til 12:15 AM EDT. Take shelter now. Check media.
CMAMlongtext: National Weather Service: TORNADO WARNING in this area until 12:15 AM EDT. Take shelter now in a basement or an interior room on the lowest floor of a sturdy building. If you are outdoors, in a mobile home, or in a vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter and protect yourself from flying debris. Check media.

properties.description:

limits, and has exited the warned area. Therefore, the warning will
be allowed to expire.

west central Ohio.

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued March 31 at 4:20PM EDT until March 31 at 4:15PM EDT by NWS Buffalo NY
properties.description:
The storm which prompted the warning has weakened below severe
limits, and no longer appears capable of producing a tornado.
Therefore, the warning has been allowed to expire.  However, small
hail and gusty winds are still possible with this thunderstorm.

A Severe Thunderstorm Watch remains in effect until 900 PM EDT for
western New York.

Remember, a Severe Thunderstorm Warning still remains in effect for
northern Allegany and far southern Livingston counties.

To report severe weather, go to our website at weather.gov/iln and
submit your report via social media, when you can do so safely.

If you are outdoors, in a mobile home, or in a vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter and protect yourself from flying debris. Check media.

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued March 26 at 7:44PM EDT until March 26 at 7:45PM EDT by NWS Indianapolis IN
properties.description:
The tornado threat has diminished and the Tornado Warning has been
cancelled. However, large hail and damaging winds remain possible
and a Severe Thunderstorm Warning remains in effect for the area.

A Severe Thunderstorm Watch remains in effect until midnight EDT for
central Indiana.

NWSheadline: THE TORNADO WARNING FOR NORTHERN MADISON AND SOUTHEASTERN TIPTON COUNTIES WILL EXPIRE AT 745 PM EDT

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued March 26 at 7:35PM EDT until March 26 at 7:45PM EDT by NWS Indianapolis IN
properties.description:
At 734 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm capable of producing a tornado
was located over Alexandria, or 10 miles south of Fairmount, moving
east at 65 mph.

HAZARD...Tornado.

SOURCE...Radar indicated rotation.

IMPACT...Flying debris will be dangerous to those caught without
shelter. Mobile homes will be damaged or destroyed. Damage
to roofs, windows, and vehicles will occur.  Tree damage is
likely.

Locations impacted include...
Elwood, Alexandria, Frankton, Summitville, and Orestes.
properties.instruction:
TAKE COVER NOW! Move to a basement or an interior room on the lowest
floor of a sturdy building. Avoid windows. If you are outdoors, in a
mobile home, or in a vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter
and protect yourself from flying debris.

properties.description:

of the warned area. Therefore, the warning will be allowed to expire.

A Tornado Watch remains in effect until 1100 PM EDT for northeastern
Ohio.

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued March 26 at 6:50PM EDT until March 26 at 7:15PM EDT by NWS Pittsburgh PA
properties.description:
At 650 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm capable of producing a tornado
was located over Sharpsville, moving southeast at 45 mph.

HAZARD...Tornado.

SOURCE...Radar indicated rotation.

IMPACT...Flying debris will be dangerous to those caught without
shelter. Mobile homes will be damaged or destroyed. Damage
to roofs, windows and vehicles will occur. Tree damage is
likely.

Locations impacted include...
Hermitage, Sharon, Grove City, Sharpsville, Farrell, New
Wilmington, Mercer, Pulaski, West Middlesex, Plain Grove Township,
Clark, Wheatland and Volant.
properties.instruction:
Move to an interior room on the lowest floor of a well-built building
away from windows. If you are outdoors, in a mobile home, or in a
vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter and protect yourself
from flying debris.

NWSheadline: TORNADO WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 715 PM EDT FOR SOUTHERN MERCER AND NORTHEASTERN LAWRENCE COUNTIES

properties.headline: Tornado Warning issued March 26 at 6:44PM EDT until March 26 at 7:00PM EDT by NWS Cleveland OH
properties.description:
At 644 PM EDT, a confirmed tornado was located near Sharpsville,
moving east at 55 mph.

HAZARD...Damaging tornado and quarter size hail.

SOURCE...Radar confirmed tornado.

IMPACT...Flying debris will be dangerous to those caught without
shelter. Mobile homes will be damaged or destroyed. Damage
to roofs, windows, and vehicles will occur.  Tree damage is
likely.

This tornadic thunderstorm will remain over mainly rural areas of
east central Trumbull County, including the following locations...
Vienna, Yankee Lake, Brookfield Center, West Hill, and Orangeville.
properties.instruction:
To repeat, a tornado is on the ground. TAKE COVER NOW! Move to a
basement or an interior room on the lowest floor of a sturdy
building. Avoid windows. If you are outdoors, in a mobile home, or in
a vehicle, move to the closest substantial shelter and protect
yourself from flying debris.

NWSheadline: A TORNADO WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 700 PM EDT FOR EAST CENTRAL TRUMBULL COUNTY
